rationale: Mongol sack of Baghdad (1258) ended the Abbasid Caliphate. Same land, new Mongolic ethnicity.
Timur conquered the post-Ilkhanate Iranian world. Same land; Timurid is Turkic vs. Mongol, but closely related.
Safavids reunified Iran after the Mongol/Timurid era. Same Iranian heartland, Persian Shia identity revived.
Safavids replaced the Timurids in Iran. Same core territories, ethnicity shifted from Turkic back to Persian.
Fatimids (Ismaili Shia) conquered Egypt from the Abbasids. Same land, rival Semitic dynasty.
Saladin (Kurdish) dismantled the Fatimid Caliphate. Same territory, Kurdish ethnicity.
Mamluk (Turkic slave-soldiers) overthrew the Ayyubids. Same territory, Turkic ethnicity.
Ottoman conquest of the Mamluk Sultanate (1517). Same territory; both Turkic, but different dynasties.
Cyrus (Persian/Achaemenid) overthrew the Medes. Both Iranian peoples on the same plateau. Near-direct lineage.
Sassanids overthrew the Parthians. Both Iranian peoples on the same core territory; Sassanids claimed Achaemenid legacy.
Arab Muslim conquest destroyed the Sassanid state. Same land, Semitic/Arab ethnicity.
Mongol invasion destroyed the Seljuk successor states in Iran. Same land, Mongol ethnicity.
Ottomans emerged from Turkic beyliks as Seljuk Rum fragmented. Same Anatolian territory, same Turkic ethnicity.
Umayyads were Arab, same Semitic ethnicity, controlled the same core territories.
Abbasid revolution replaced Umayyads. Still Arab/Semitic but shifted capital to Baghdad.
Seljuk Turks became the military power behind the weakened Abbasid caliphate. Same land, Turkic ethnicity.
A Seljuk branch migrated into Anatolia and founded the Sultanate of Rum. Same Turkic ethnicity, new territory.
